teamsLeaderForms.iss:
Each Sunday, requires 35-40 individuals to serve all 3 services. 

One of the biggest challenges to getting people to serve are their schedules that conflict with serving or even attending church, and several people deciding not to serve for long seasons. Right now we have 20%  of people serving consistently doing 80% of the work. We currently have 85-90 active members with many of them teenagers or members not ready or desiring to lead a class. This brings the number of “teachers” or class leaders closer to 19 including Deana and myself. We need 14 lead guides or teachers each Sunday. 

Our goal is to have members serve twice a month. At around 90 active members and only 19 lead guides were not operating in a capacity that offers sustainability or growth. If we had 150 members, that would nearly double our current ministry size which would work with people’s lifestyle preferences outside of church.

I would rather have a smaller dedicated team who is truly filled with the spirit but that will take time.
teamsLeaderForms.more: Other than what’s mentioned above, our onboarding process needs an overhaul with short term needs that are more urgent and long term goals that I believe will lead to growth and sustainability that will allow stepping stones to flourish.
teamsLeaderForms.res: Not that I can think of. Trying to balance starting a daycare and the demands of where our ministry is at now is challenge. Stepping Stones feels like it’s on life support and not self sustaining. This question has been on my heart lately: What’s the greater focus and what does God want us to focus on in this season. Is it the health of ministry and daycare, is it ministry first then daycare, something I’ve been asking myself because I’m beginning to see more need in leading the stepping stones ministry.
teamsLeaderForms.suc: The only person I see now is Whitney Maidens but I sense there is someone who just joined or will join soon who may be that person. Whitney can do it and I think she’s wonderful. The question I ask is who do we feel God is calling to do it. Who has a burning desire to lead this ministry to where God wants it. For now, I believe I am that person, but I’ve always replaced myself in my previous career, so I am constantly looking for who has the hunger and drive. I look forward to the season where I’m scouting our next leaders and they don’t have to replace me, we can begin to create new positions for roles that will prepare people to lead as a director and create avenues for growth.

teamsLeaderForms.iss:
2 ppl are needed per week = 8 to 10 ppl/month
If we have 4 more ppl we could give the option to serve once every six or seven weeks! Yay happy servers!! :-)
teamsLeaderForms.more: Trying to present and to have a clean coffee table at all times and for all the services. Ppl and kids not putting trash in the trash can.
teamsLeaderForms.res: Not sure what team sets up the trash cans in the mornings but we need to have two trash cans by the coffee table every week, thank you!

teamsLeaderForms.iss: We need 2 per transition period. between 8 & 9:30 and 9:30 & 11. We also need one after the 11 as well, so that's 5 per Sunday. Currently, we have one volunteer wanting to serve every Sunday, another willing to serve all services once a month, and everyone else willing to serve 2x a month, so we don't need all 20 total, but thinking ahead, this is the ideal number.
teamsLeaderForms.suc: Tim Frederick  was leading, struggled with scheduling system and availability. Tried to hand off to Bill Wallace, didn't want to deal with the scheduling system. Michael France was interested but he's not a member and won't get baptized yet for some reason. Need new blood, younger, willing to learn and lead with PCO.

teamsLeaderForms.iss: There are 24-30 services each month to be prayed over. We have lost members due to moves or just bad physical health. Some serve only one service a month and others 2-3. My desire is to get more young people involved.
teamsLeaderForms.more: I need help invigorating the team. I need to have a meeting, but I would love to have a few new members to introduce to the team.
teamsLeaderForms.res: Some of my older members are not technically savvy and have a hard time with the app. I have to text them separately what I post on the app.

teamsLeaderForms.iss: We currently have 51 volunteers serving communion, and we need an additional 34 to stay fully staffed. With summer approaching, many are unavailable due to vacations, illness and other health issues, kids’ sports schedules, travel for events, camping, and family commitments. We’re also seeing conflicts from work schedules, last-minute obligations, and a general seasonal drop in availability.
teamsLeaderForms.more: Nothing major, just occasional scheduling conflicts, but I’ll communicate ahead of time if anything comes up.
teamsLeaderForms.res: Clear communication and advance notice of speacial tasks our team needs to perform and be ready for-always helpful, but otherwise I’m all set.
teamsLeaderForms.suc:
Cindy Lefner- already trained
Jenn Jauron- already trained 
Melissa Ayrault- already trained

teamsLeaderForms.iss: It would be good to have 4 people serving in the church per service, walking around, welcoming, and connecting with people. Also, it will help us have some presents when people can't make it at the last minute.

To have 2 at the desk at each service
To have enough people to cover sickness and vacations

We need 2 more on the tech team to fill out proper rotation and backups.
We need 10 or more on the worship team. Mostly Instrumentalists. 12 of our 16-member worship team are vocalists. We desperately need more instrumentalists. I'm flabbergasted that, of almost 1000 people coming every Sunday, we have 3 guitarists.
teamsLeaderForms.more: Lack of passionate/skilled volunteers is my biggest issue.
teamsLeaderForms.res: A dedicated tech director would do a lot for the ministry as a whole.
teamsLeaderForms.suc: I had someone I was working with, but they are now at the Midtown Church. I have one young person that I'm trying to mentor into a band leader. Adelyn Gibson.

teamsLeaderForms.iss: There are about 8 people who are on the team but not currently serving. Need to make up for those not serving.
teamsLeaderForms.more: A number of people not serving and getting people to sign up on a regular basis. U I

teamsLeaderForms.iss: As we grow our need grows. We have some individuals who are interested in serving with students that I will be meeting with in the next week or so.
teamsLeaderForms.res: A space that doesn’t need to be set up and torn down every week. A safer place for students to interact and play that isn’t in a parking lot. We are thriving with what we have but it can only get better when we have the opportunity for our own space.

teamsLeaderForms.iss: We have a good flow right now and I don’t think anyone is over burdened by the frequency of serving.
I want to have a team lead and assistant for all your services. This will allow them to manage the teams better.
teamsLeaderForms.more: Just getting more people to serve
teamsLeaderForms.suc: Im the team overseer and have built up 6 leaders and have added another who confirmed on Sunday. Tim Martin wants to be a leader.
